svg.text: Build H first, then W from H · single slice shown · the dashed loop wraps the whole flow for 4D stacks
4D outer loop · repeats per slice ◀▶ 3D: run once
H seed is fixed across slices, captured from the display slice
① Build the H seed from fixed inputs
② Fill any empty H rows from the spectral-information table

g.text: For components that have no ROI yet:
Average of the resonance slices for this
↪ Kept as final W if Overwrite = OFF

svg.text: ③ optional: Build W from H

tspan.style: font-style:normal;font-variant:normal;font-weight:bold;font-stretch:normal;font-family:'-apple-system', 'Segoe UI', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif;-inkscape-font-specification:'-apple-system, Segoe UI, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif Bold'

text.tspan: When is this step actually needed?

g.text: • Seeded NMMF: optional ("Overwrite spectral W map with H"— only a starting point.
The solver will rescale W and H freely anyway.
• Fixed-H NNLS: required — this W is THE result.

svg.text: Seed W₀ (spatial maps · one image per component)
④ Fill any missing user seeds from the data residual

g.text: Residual H for the missing components
Subtract everything the existing H rows already explain. The leftover signal is averaged across the strongest residual
pixels, smoothed, and rescaled to the existing H amplitude — that's the new H row. Then build its W with the chosen mode.

svg.text: H + W passed to the chosen solver
4D loop variants — what stays fixed, what changes

g.text: H seed restored from display slice;
full NMMF runs on the slice's own data.
Both H and W adapt to each slice.
Best when spectra are expected to
Display slice: full NMMF → fixed H_ref
Other slices: NNLS with H = H_ref
Re-runs steps ①–④ per slice against
the display-slice H. W ← NNLS abundance.
W is the per-slice abundance map —
★ H first, W after. Step ③ is just initialization for seeded NMMF (solver rescales anyway) but produces the actual abundance result for Fixed-H NNLS.
★ W is normalized to peak=1 for seeded NMMF; in Fixed-H NNLS the nnls mode keeps the absolute abundance because that map IS the result. The exponential weighting is only in h_weighted.

svg.text: ② Fill W columns from the spectral-information table
